reconstructed_sketch: From chapter context alone (2.49 ranks buddhi-yoga above karma; 2.51 names the destination padam anāmayam), an analog at position 2.50 might address the mechanism by which buddhi-yoga releases its binding. But the specific contribution of 2.50 — the DEFINITION of yoga as kauśalam (skill in action), the surprising dual-shedding of BOTH sukṛta and duṣkṛte — would not be reconstructible from chapter alone. From the chapter context one might guess 'discipline that releases binding'; only with 2.50's specific Sanskrit (yogaḥ karmasu kauśalam, ubhe sukṛta-duṣkṛte, buddhi-yukta) would the analog know to (a) define yoga as skill (not effort or attitude), (b) name the dual-shedding as a structural surprise, (c) operationalize as artifact-density.
verdict_reason: The verse adds strong specific signal not reconstructible from chapter alone. Score 1/10 — the chapter context gives only the general 'discipline' theme; the verse's specific contributions (definitional kauśalam, dual-shedding, artifact-density) are 2.50-specific. Pass.
